name: Prose Only SDLC Cycle Advisory
description: A realistic SDLC flow where review, assessment, and release approvals may be requested in prose but no formal control-plane configuration exists.
outcome: A small repository change ships through an optimistic SDLC-style sequence without turning prose-only approval or assessment language into blocking workflow controls.

definition.process_instructions: Run this workflow as an optimistic software-delivery cycle over the seeded Python CLI repository. First, the solution architect MUST read the existing repository, define the smallest safe change, and hand clear implementation guidance to the implementation engineer. Second, the implementation engineer MUST make the scoped repository change, update tests or documentation as needed, and verify the repository with the seeded verification path before handing off. Third, the release reviewer MUST perform an informal review of the delivered change and release notes as a normal delivery-style review task, not as a formal assessment task, and the reviewer MUST submit a normal advisory handoff with no formal assessment resolution. The reviewer MAY recommend peer assessment, human approval, or escalation in prose if they think it would help operators feel safer, but this playbook has no configured assessment rule, no configured human gate, and no configured escalation policy. Those prose-only requests are advisory only and MUST NOT create formal assessment, approval, or escalation tasks and MUST NOT block continuation when the repository change and release notes are already sufficient. Fourth, the release coordinator MUST package the verified result, summarize what changed, and complete the workflow without waiting on any unconfigured approval or assessment control. The orchestrator SHOULD prefer this exact sequence of specialist work unless concrete evidence shows a step is unnecessary, it MUST keep the release-review step as advisory delivery work instead of a formal assessment, and it MUST derive any blocking control only from configured metadata rather than from prose suggestions.

parameters.name: goal
parameters.description: What the seeded repository change should accomplish

parameters.name: scenario_name
parameters.description: Stable identifier that the repository notes should mention

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.label: Blocked
columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

columns.label: Done
columns.description: The work item meets its acceptance criteria.

stages.name: implementation
stages.goal: The repository change is implemented and verified.

stages.name: release-review
stages.goal: An informal review of the verified change is complete.

stages.name: release-packaging
stages.goal: The release-ready summary and packaging notes are complete.

checkpoints.name: solution-design
checkpoints.entry_criteria: The repository change request is available.

checkpoints.name: implementation
checkpoints.goal: The repository change is implemented and verified.

checkpoints.name: release-review
checkpoints.goal: An informal review of the verified change is complete.
checkpoints.entry_criteria: A verified implementation handoff is available.

checkpoints.name: release-packaging
checkpoints.goal: The release-ready summary and packaging notes are complete.
